## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for reading and writing to .pen files and gives you tools to be an expert design generator and validator specifically for web and mobile applications and websites.

- IMPORTANT: The contents of the .pen files are encrypted and can be only access via the "pencil" MCP tools. DO NOT use "Read" or "Grep" tools to read contents of .pen files.
- IMPORTANT: Make sure to ONLY use the "pencil" MCP tools when reading or searching contents of .pen files (batch_get) and making changes inside .pen files (batch_design).
- IMPORTANT: Always follow the tool definitions and formats for the "pencil" MCP tools.

Available tools:
- "get_editor_state({ include_schema })": Start with this tool if you are unaware of the current editor state. Use this tool to determine in the beginning of a task, to understand what is the currently active .pen file you want to work on and the current user selection in it and other essential context information.
- "open_document(filePathOrNew)": When there is no active editor opened, pass 'new' to create an empty .pen file or if the user explicitly ask to open a specific .pen file use its path to open it.
- "get_guidelines(category?, name?, params?)": Load guides and styles for working with .pen files.
- "batch_get(patterns, nodeIds)": Retrieve nodes by searching for matching patterns or by reading node ids in batches. Use this for discovering and understand .pen files.
- "batch_design(operations)": Use it when designing with .pen files to execute multiple insert/copy/update/replace/move/delete/image operations in a single call. IMPORTANT: MAKE SURE TO FOLLOW THE OPERATION SYNTAX DEFINED IN THE TOOL'S DESCRIPTION. Aim for maximum 25 operations per call—enough to make meaningful progress without overwhelming the system. Available operations (used as a small script where every line a single operation call):
	- Insert: foo=I("parent", { ... })
	- Copy: baz=C("nodeid", "parent", { ...})
	- Replace: foo2=R("nodeid1/nodeid2", {...})
	- Update: U(foo+"/nodeid", {...})
	- Dele… [truncated]

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).

content.content:
1	# Cairn
2	
3	A persistent memory plugin for Claude Code. Markdown vault maintained by
4	your agent across sessions. Based on [Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern](https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f).
5	
6	## What it does
7	
8	- **SessionStart hook** injects working set, index, and recent session summaries or manifests (priority order)
9	- **Stop hook** writes a small session manifest when you finish working
10	- **PostCompact hook** re-injects vault context after compaction (solves compaction amnesia)
11	- **CAIRN.md template** teaches Claude to ingest sources, answer queries, and lint your vault
12	
13	## Install
14	
15	```bash
16	bunx cairn init
17	```
18	
19	Scaffolds `~/cairn` with vault structure and registers hooks in Claude Code.
20	Safe to run again — idempotent.
21	
22	### Custom vault path
23	
24	```bash
25	bunx cairn init --vault-path /path/to/vault
26	```
27	
28	### Per-project vault
29	
30	Create a `.cairn` file in your project root containing the vault path:
31	
32	```
33	/path/to/project/vault
34	```
35	
36	## Usage
37	
38	### Ingest
39	Give Claude any source — file path, URL, pasted text, or conversation context:
40	
41	> "Ingest this article on dependency injection"
42	
43	Claude reads source, copies files to `raw/` for provenance, presents
44	takeaways for confirmation, writes wiki pages, cascades updates to related
45	pages, updates index and log. Single ingest typically touches 5-15 pages.
46	
47	### Query
48	Ask Claude anything your vault might know:
49	
50	> "What decisions did we make about the auth system?"
51	
52	Claude checks index, follows wikilinks, answers with citations. Novel
53	insights get filed as new wiki pages automatically.
54	
55	### Lint
56	Ask Claude to check vault health:
57	
58	> "Lint the vault"
59	
60	Reports orphan pages, dead links, missing frontmatter, stale content,
61	missing types, and contradictions.
62	
63	### Refine
64	Ask Claude to improve vault structure:
65	
66	> "Refine the vault"
67	
68	Finds stale pages, under-connected pages, merge/split candidates, and
69	backlink gaps. Presents findings for approval, then applies changes.
70	Shows vault health before and after.
71	
72	### Extract
73	Extract knowledge from session manifests into wiki pages:
74	
75	> "Extract from sessions"
76	
77	Sessions are sources. Claude asks `cairn summarize` to lazily create cached
78	summaries for unprocessed manifests, presents extraction candidates (entities,
79	concepts, decisions, patterns), and runs the standard ingest workflow on
80	confirmed items.
81	
82	Toggle session-start nudge: `/cairn:extract on` or `/cairn:extract off`.
83	
84	## Page types
85	
86	Every wiki page has a `type` in frontmatter:
87	
88	| Type | Purpose |
89	|------|---------|
90	| `concept` | Ideas, techniques, patterns |
91	| `entity` | People, orgs, tools, projects |
92	| `source-summary` | Digest of a `raw/` document |
93	| `comparison` | X vs Y trade-off analysis |
94	| `overview` | Guided index of a topic area |
95	
96	Each type has a structural template defined in CAIRN.md with recommended sections.
97	
98	## Vault structure
99	
100	```
101	~/cairn/
102	  CAIRN.md        # Schema — conventions, workflows, rules
103	  context.md      # Working set — current focus areas (injected first)
104	  index.md        # Categorized pointer index, grouped by topic
105	  log.md          # Chronological operation log (heading-level entries)
106	  wiki/           # Knowledge pages (agent-maintained, typed)
107	  raw/            # Source documents (immutable, provenance)
108	  sessions/       # Session manifests (auto-generated)
109	    summaries/    # Cached summaries from manifests
110	    .trash/       # Migration quarantine and non-destructive replacements
111	```
112	
113	## Context injection
114	
115	Inject hook reads vault in priority order under a 32KB budget (configurable
116	via `CAIRN_BUDGET` env var; `cairn doctor` warns when the budget is under
117	pressure):
118	
119	1. **`context.md`** — working set, always first
120	2. **`index.md`** — categorized page index
121	3. **Recent sessions** — cached summaries first, then manifests as fallback
122	
123	## Session migration
124	
125	Cairn v0.6.0 stores session manifests separately from cached summaries. Existing
126	vaults can preview and apply the one-time migration:
127	
128	```bash
129	bunx cairn migrate-sessions
130	bunx cairn migrate-sessions --apply --yes
131	```
132	
133	`cairn doctor` warns when legacy session files remain and reports manifest,
134	summary, and trash counts.
135	
136	## Search (optional)
137	
138	For larger vaults, [qmd](https://github.com/qntx-labs/qmd) provides
139	BM25 + vector hybrid search over markdown files.
140	
141	```bash
142	# Install
143	npm install -g @tobilu/qmd
144	
145	# Register vault
146	qmd collection add ~/cairn --name cairn --mask "**/*.md"
147	qmd embed
148	
149	# Add MCP server to Claude Code
150	# In your Claude Code MCP config:
151	# { "mcpServers": { "qmd": { "command": "qmd", "args": ["mcp"] } } }
152	```
153	
154	When qmd MCP tools are available, Query and Refine workflows use
155	`qmd_deep_search` before falling back to manual index reading.
156	
157	## Model choice
158	
159	Stop hooks do not call an LLM. Summaries are generated on demand by
160	`cairn summarize`, which calls `claude -p --model haiku` by default. For tests
161	or custom integrations, set `CAIRN_SUMMARIZE_COMMAND` to a compatible command.
162	
163	## Uninstall
164	
165	```bash
166	bunx cairn uninstall
167	```
168	
169	Removes hooks from Claude Code. Your vault is preserved.
170	
171	## License
172	
173	MIT
174

# Cairn

A persistent memory plugin for Claude Code. Markdown vault maintained by
your agent across sessions. Based on [Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern](https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f).

## What it does

- **SessionStart hook** injects working set, index, and recent session summaries or manifests (priority order)
- **Stop hook** writes a small session manifest when you finish working
- **PostCompact hook** re-injects vault context after compaction (solves compaction amnesia)
- **CAIRN.md template** teaches Claude to ingest sources, answer queries, and lint your vault

## Install

```bash
bunx cairn init
```

Scaffolds `~/cairn` with vault structure and registers hooks in Claude Code.
Safe to run again — idempotent.

### Custom vault path

```bash
bunx cairn init --vault-path /path/to/vault
```

### Per-project vault

Create a `.cairn` file in your project root containing the vault path:

```
/path/to/project/vault
```

## Usage

### Ingest
Give Claude any source — file path, URL, pasted text, or conversation context:

> "Ingest this article on dependency injection"

Claude reads source, copies files to `raw/` for provenance, presents
takeaways for confirmation, writes wiki pages, cascades updates to related
pages, updates index and log. Single ingest typically touches 5-15 pages.

### Query
Ask Claude anything your vault might know:

> "What decisions did we make about the auth system?"

Claude checks index, follows wikilinks, answers with citations. Novel
insights get filed as new wiki pages automatically.

### Lint
Ask Claude to check vault health:

> "Lint the vault"

Reports orphan pages, dead links, missing frontmatter, stale content,
missing types, and contradictions.

### Refine
Ask Claude to improve vault structure:

> "Refine the vault"

Finds stale pages, under-connected pages, merge/split candidates, and
backlink gaps. Presents findings for approval, then applies changes.
Shows vault health before and after.

### Extract
Extract knowledge from session manifests into wiki pages:

> "Extract from sessions"

Sessions are sources. Claude asks `cairn summarize` to lazily create cached
summaries for unprocessed manifests, presents extraction candidates (entities,
concepts, decisions, patterns), and runs the standard ingest workflow on
confirmed items.

Toggle session-start nudge: `/cairn:extract on` or `/cairn:extract off`.

## Page types

Every wiki page has a `type` in frontmatter:

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `concept` | Ideas, techniques, patterns |
| `entity` | People, orgs, tools, projects |
| `source-summary` | Digest of a `raw/` document |
| `comparison` | X vs Y trade-off analysis |
| `overview` | Guided index of a topic area |

Each type has a structural template defined in CAIRN.md with recommended sections.

## Vault structure

```
~/cairn/
  CAIRN.md        # Schema — conventions, workflows, rules
  context.md      # Working set — current focus areas (injected first)
  index.md        # Categorized pointer index, grouped by topic
  log.md          # Chronological operation log (heading-level entries)
  wiki/           # Knowledge pages (agent-maintained, typed)
  raw/            # Source documents (immutable, provenance)
  sessions/       # Session manifests (auto-generated)
    summaries/    # Cached summaries from manifests
    .trash/       # Migration quarantine and non-destructive replacements
```

## Context injection

Inject hook reads vault in priority order under a 32KB budget (configurable
via `CAIRN_BUDGET` env var; `cairn doctor` warns when the budget is under
pressure):

1. **`context.md`** — working set, always first
2. **`index.md`** — categorized page index
3. **Recent sessions** — cached summaries first, then manifests as fallback

## Session migration

Cairn v0.6.0 stores session manifests separately from cached summaries. Existing
vaults can preview and apply the one-time migration:

```bash
bunx cairn migrate-sessions
bunx cairn migrate-sessions --apply --yes
```

`cairn doctor` warns when legacy session files remain and reports manifest,
summary, and trash counts.

## Search (optional)

For larger vaults, [qmd](https://github.com/qntx-labs/qmd) provides
BM25 + vector hybrid search over markdown files.

```bash
# Install
npm install -g @tobilu/qmd

# Register vault
qmd collection add ~/cairn --name cairn --mask "**/*.md"
qmd embed

# Add MCP server to Claude Code
# In your Claude Code MCP config:
# { "mcpServers": { "qmd": { "command": "qmd", "args": ["mcp"] } } }
```

When qmd MCP tools are available, Query and Refine workflows use
`qmd_deep_search` before falling back to manual index reading.

## Model choice

Stop hooks do not call an LLM. Summaries are generated on demand by
`cairn summarize`, which calls `claude -p --model haiku` by default. For tests
or custom integrations, set `CAIRN_SUMMARIZE_COMMAND` to a compatible command.

## Uninstall

```bash
bunx cairn uninstall
```

Removes hooks from Claude Code. Your vault is preserved.

## License

MIT

197	## Markdown Conventions
198	
199	### Wikilinks
200	Use `[[wikilinks]]` for all internal references:
201	- `[[Page Name]]` — link to page
202	- `[[Page Name|Display Text]]` — aliased link
203	- `[[Page Name#Heading]]` — link to heading
204	- `[[Page Name#^block-id]]` — link to block
205	
206	### Embeds and Callouts
207	- Embed a page: `![[Page Name]]`
208	- Embed a heading: `![[Page Name#Heading]]`
209	- Callouts: `> [!info]`, `> [!warning]`, `> [!tip]`
210	
211	## Workflows
212	
213	### Ingest
214	
215	When the user asks you to ingest something (file path, URL, pasted text, or conversation context):
216	
217	1. Read the source from wherever it lives — a local path, URL, or inline content.
218	2. If the source is a file, copy it to `raw/` for provenance (preserving original filename).
219	3. **Present key takeaways to the user**: entities found, concepts identified, relationships discovered, and any contradictions with existing wiki content. Confirm which are worth filing before proceeding.
220	4. For each confirmed entity/concept, create or update a wiki page in `wiki/`.
221	5. **Review all existing wiki pages that relate to this source.** Update them with new cross-references, corrections, or additional context from this source. A single ingest typically touches 5-15 existing pages.
222	6. Link related pages with `[[wikilinks]]` — every page links to at least 2 others.
223	7. Update `## Backlinks` sections on all pages you linked to — add an entry with context for each new inbound link.
224	8. Update `context.md` if the ingested material relates to current focus areas.
225	9. Add entries to `index.md` under the appropriate category.
226	10. Append to `log.md`:
227	
228	```markdown
229	## [YYYY-MM-DD] ingest | <source title>
230	
231	Created [[Page A]], [[Page B]]. Updated [[Page C]], [[Page D]], [[Page E]].
232	```
233	
234	### Query
235	
236	When the user asks a question the vault might answer:
237	
238	1. Search for relevant pages: use `qmd_deep_search` if available, otherwise read `index.md`.
239	2. Follow `[[wikilinks]]` to read related pages.
240	3. Synthesize your answer, citing sources as `[[Page Name]]`.
241	4. If your answer contains novel knowledge worth keeping, write a new wiki page and add it to `index.md`.
242	5. Append to `log.md`:
243	
244	```markdown
245	## [YYYY-MM-DD] query | <brief question summary>
246	
247	Synthesized from [[Page A]], [[Page B]]. Created [[Page C]] (if applicable).
248	```
249	
250	### Lint
251	
252	When the user asks you to lint the vault:
253	
254	1. **Vault health dashboard**: Before listing findings, compute and display:
255	   ```
256	   ## Vault Health
257	   - Pages: N | Links: N | Avg links/page: N.N
258	   - Orphans: N | Dead links: N | Stale (>30d): N
259	   - Types: concept(N) entity(N) source-summary(N) comparison(N) overview(N)
260	   - Backlinks coverage: N/N pages (N%)
261	   ```
262	2. **Orphan pages**: wiki pages with no inbound links from other wiki pages or index.md.
263	3. **Dead links**: `[[wikilinks]]` pointing to non-existent pages.
264	4. **Missing frontmatter**: wiki pages without required YAML frontmatter fields (`title`, `type`, `created`, `updated`, `tags`).
265	5. **Stale content**: pages with `updated` date older than 30 days.
266	6. **Missing types**: wiki pages without a valid `type` field.
267	7. **Contradictions**: claims in one wiki page that conflict with claims in another. Flag both pages and the conflicting statements. Contradictions are the most dangerous vault failure mode.
268	8. **Missing backlinks**: wiki pages without a `## Backlinks` section, or pages whose backlinks are out of sync with actual inbound wikilinks.
269	9. Report all findings. All fixes are opt-in — do not auto-fix without user approval.
270	
271	### Refine
272	
273	When the user asks you to refine the vault (or runs `/cairn:refine`):
274	
275	1. Run the vault health dashboard (same as lint step 1) to establish baseline.
276	2. **Stale pages**: find pages with `updated` older than 30 days. For each, check if the content is still accurate. Present stale pages to the user with a recommendation: update, archive, or leave.
277	3. **Under-connected pages**: find pages with fewer than 3 inbound links (check `## Backlinks` sections). Suggest connections to related pages.
278	4. **Merge candidates**: find pages covering overlapping topics (similar tags, significant wikilink overlap). Suggest merges — present both pages and a proposed combined structure.
279	5. **Split candidates**: find pages covering multiple distinct topics (multiple H2 sections with unrelated content). Suggest splits.
280	6. **Backlinks audit**: update `## Backlinks` sections across the vault to match actual wikilinks.
281	7. Apply user-approved changes. Update `index.md` and `context.md` as needed.
282	8. Run the vault health dashboard again to show improvement.
283	9. Append to `log.md`:
284	
285	```markdown
286	## [YYYY-MM-DD] refine | vault refinement pass
287	
288	Baseline: N pages, N.N avg links. Updated [[Page A]], merged [[Page B]] + [[Page C]], split [[Page D]]. Result: N pages, N.N avg links.
289	```
290	
291	## Index Format
292	
293	`index.md` groups pages by topic category, newest first within each category:
294	
295	```markdown
296	# Vault Index
297	
298	## Architecture
299	- [[Layered Architecture]] — Handler → Service → Repo/Gateway boundaries
300	- [[Dependency Injection]] — Constructor-based DI pattern for Go services
301	
302	## Tools
303	- [[React 19]] — New features: use hook, actions, compiler
304	- [[Stripe]] — Payment gateway integration patterns
305	```
306	
307	Categories emerge organically during ingest. Create new categories as needed. Each entry: `- [[Page Name]] — one-line description` (~150 chars max).
308	
309	## Log Format
310	
311	`log.md` uses heading-level entries for Obsidian folding and CLI parsing:
312	
313	```markdown
314	## [YYYY-MM-DD] type | description
315	
316	Details of what was created/updated.
317	```
318	
319	Types: `ingest`, `query`, `lint`, `refine`, `session` (lowercase).
320	
321	## Working Set
322	
323	`context.md` tracks current focus areas. The inject hook reads this first — it gets highest priority in the context budget.
324	
325	```markdown
326	# Working Set
327	
328	## Active
329	- [[Page Name]] — why it matters right now
330	
331	## Background
332	- [[Page Name]] — reference material for active work
333	```
334	
335	Update `context.md` when:
336	- The user starts or finishes a major work area
337	- Ingested material relates to current focus
338	- The user explicitly asks to shift focus
339	
340	## Search
341	
342	When [qmd](https://github.com/qntx-labs/qmd) is available (via MCP tools `qmd_search`, `qmd_deep_search`, `qmd_get`), use it as the primary search mechanism for Query and Refine workflows.
343	
344	### Setup (user responsibility)
345	
346	1. Install qmd: `npm install -g @tobilu/qmd`
347	2. Register the vault: `qmd collection add ~/cairn --name cairn --mask "**/*.md"`
348	3. Generate embeddings: `qmd embed`
349	4. Add MCP server to Claude Code config:
350	   ```json
351	   {
352	     "mcpServers": {
353	       "qmd": { "command": "qmd", "args": ["mcp"] }
354	     }
355	   }
356	   ```
357	
358	### Usage in workflows
359	
360	Before each workflow, check your actual tool list for `mcp__qmd__qmd_search`,
361	`mcp__qmd__qmd_deep_search`, and `mcp__qmd__qmd_get` (or the non-prefixed
362	`qmd_*` form). Decide from presence, not from memory.
363	
364	When the tools are present:
365	- **Query**: Use `qmd_deep_search` first, then `qmd_get` for top results, then follow wikilinks.
366	- **Refine**: Use `qmd_search` to find pages with overlapping content (merge candidates).
367	- **Ingest cascade**: Use `qmd_search` to find related pages that need updating.
368	
369	When the tools are absent, fall back to reading `index.md` and following
370	wikilinks manually. Announce the fallback once per session. qmd is optional —
371	the vault works without it.
372	
373	## Rules
374	
375	1. **Never modify files in `raw/`.** They are archived originals preserved for provenance.
376	2. **Every wiki page links to 2+ related pages.** Isolated pages are less useful.
377	3. **Frontmatter on every wiki page.** Required: `title`, `type`, `created`, `updated`, `tags`.
378	4. **Use the correct page type.** Match content to the five defined types.
379	5. **Discuss before filing.** Present takeaways to the user before creating pages during ingest.
380	6. **Cascade updates.** When ingesting, review and update all related existing pages.
381	7. **Categorize the index.** Group entries by topic domain, not flat chronological.
382	8. **Heading-level log entries.** Use `## [YYYY-MM-DD] type | description` format.
383	9. **Maintain the working set.** Keep `context.md` current with active focus areas.
384	10. **Skeptical memory.** Before acting on any recalled fact, verify it against the current codebase or source. Memory is a hint, not truth.
385	11. **Atomic pages.** One concept per wiki page. If a page covers multiple topics, split it.
386	12. **Maintain backlinks.** Every wiki page has a `## Backlinks` section at the bottom listing pages that link to it, with context. Update backlinks on the target page whenever you create or update a wikilink.

3. **Present key takeaways to the user**: entities found, concepts identified, relationships discovered, and any contradictions with existing wiki content. Confirm which are worth filing before proceeding.
4. For each confirmed entity/concept, create or update a wiki page in `wiki/`.
5. **Review all existing wiki pages that relate to this source.** Update them with new cross-references, corrections, or additional context from this source. A single ingest typically touches 5-15 existing pages.
6. Link related pages with `[[wikilinks]]` — every page links to at least 2 others.
7. Update `## Backlinks` sections on all pages you linked to — add an entry with context for each new inbound link.
8. Update `context.md` if the ingested material relates to current focus areas.
9. Add entries to `index.md` under the appropriate category.
10. Append to `log.md`:

```markdown
## [YYYY-MM-DD] ingest | <source title>

Created [[Page A]], [[Page B]]. Updated [[Page C]], [[Page D]], [[Page E]].
```

### Query

When the user asks a question the vault might answer:

1. Search for relevant pages: use `qmd_deep_search` if available, otherwise read `index.md`.
2. Follow `[[wikilinks]]` to read related pages.
3. Synthesize your answer, citing sources as `[[Page Name]]`.
4. If your answer contains novel knowledge worth keeping, write a new wiki page and add it to `index.md`.
5. Append to `log.md`:

```markdown
## [YYYY-MM-DD] query | <brief question summary>

Synthesized from [[Page A]], [[Page B]]. Created [[Page C]] (if applicable).
```

### Lint

When the user asks you to lint the vault:

1. **Vault health dashboard**: Before listing findings, compute and display:
   ```
   ## Vault Health
   - Pages: N | Links: N | Avg links/page: N.N
   - Orphans: N | Dead links: N | Stale (>30d): N
   - Types: concept(N) entity(N) source-summary(N) comparison(N) overview(N)
   - Backlinks coverage: N/N pages (N%)
   ```
2. **Orphan pages**: wiki pages with no inbound links from other wiki pages or index.md.
3. **Dead links**: `[[wikilinks]]` pointing to non-existent pages.
4. **Missing frontmatter**: wiki pages without required YAML frontmatter fields (`title`, `type`, `created`, `updated`, `tags`).
5. **Stale content**: pages with `updated` date older than 30 days.
6. **Missing types**: wiki pages without a valid `type` field.
7. **Contradictions**: claims in one wiki page that conflict with claims in another. Flag both pages and the conflicting statements. Contradictions are the most dangerous vault failure mode.
8. **Missing backlinks**: wiki pages without a `## Backlinks` section, or pages whose backlinks are out of sync with actual inbound wikilinks.
9. Report all findings. All fixes are opt-in — do not auto-fix without user approval.

### Refine

When the user asks you to refine the vault (or runs `/cairn:refine`):

1. Run the vault health dashboard (same as lint step 1) to establish baseline.
2. **Stale pages**: find pages with `updated` older than 30 days. For each, check if the content is still accurate. Present stale pages to the user with a recommendation: update, archive, or leave.
3. **Under-connected pages**: find pages with fewer than 3 inbound links (check `## Backlinks` sections). Suggest connections to related pages.
4. **Merge candidates**: find pages covering overlapping topics (similar tags, significant wikilink overlap). Suggest merges — present both pages and a proposed combined structure.
5. **Split candidates**: find pages covering multiple distinct topics (multiple H2 sections with unrelated content). Suggest splits.
6. **Backlinks audit**: update `## Backlinks` sections across the vault to match actual wikilinks.
7. Apply user-approved changes. Update `index.md` and `context.md` as needed.
8. Run the vault health dashboard again to show improvement.
9. Append to `log.md`:

```markdown
## [YYYY-MM-DD] refine | vault refinement pass

Baseline: N pages, N.N avg links. Updated [[Page A]], merged [[Page B]] + [[Page C]], split [[Page D]]. Result: N pages, N.N avg links.
```

## Index Format

`index.md` groups pages by topic category, newest first within each category:

```markdown
# Vault Index

## Architecture
- [[Layered Architecture]] — Handler → Service → Repo/Gateway boundaries
- [[Dependency Injection]] — Constructor-based DI pattern for Go services

## Tools
- [[React 19]] — New features: use hook, actions, compiler
- [[Stripe]] — Payment gateway integration patterns
```

Categories emerge organically during ingest. Create new categories as needed. Each entry: `- [[Page Name]] — one-line description` (~150 chars max).

## Log Format

`log.md` uses heading-level entries for Obsidian folding and CLI parsing:

```markdown
## [YYYY-MM-DD] type | description

Details of what was created/updated.
```

Types: `ingest`, `query`, `lint`, `refine`, `session` (lowercase).

## Working Set

`context.md` tracks current focus areas. The inject hook reads this first — it gets highest priority in the context budget.

```markdown
# Working Set

## Active
- [[Page Name]] — why it matters right now

## Background
- [[Page Name]] — reference material for active work
```

Update `context.md` when:
- The user starts or finishes a major work area
- Ingested material relates to current focus
- The user explicitly asks to shift focus

## Search

When [qmd](https://github.com/qntx-labs/qmd) is available (via MCP tools `qmd_search`, `qmd_deep_search`, `qmd_get`), use it as the primary search mechanism for Query and Refine workflows.

### Setup (user responsibility)

1. Install qmd: `npm install -g @tobilu/qmd`
2. Register the vault: `qmd collection add ~/cairn --name cairn --mask "**/*.md"`
3. Generate embeddings: `qmd embed`
4. Add MCP server to Claude Code config:
   ```json
   {
     "mcpServers": {
       "qmd": { "command": "qmd", "args": ["mcp"] }
     }
   }
   ```

### Usage in workflows

Before each workflow, check your actual tool list for `mcp__qmd__qmd_search`,
`mcp__qmd__qmd_deep_search`, and `mcp__qmd__qmd_get` (or the non-prefixed
`qmd_*` form). Decide from presence, not from memory.

When the tools are present:
- **Query**: Use `qmd_deep_search` first, then `qmd_get` for top results, then follow wikilinks.
- **Refine**: Use `qmd_search` to find pages with overlapping content (merge candidates).
- **Ingest cascade**: Use `qmd_search` to find related pages that need updating.

When the tools are absent, fall back to reading `index.md` and following
wikilinks manually. Announce the fallback once per session. qmd is optional —
the vault works without it.

## Rules

1. **Never modify files in `raw/`.** They are archived originals preserved for provenance.
2. **Every wiki page links to 2+ related pages.** Isolated pages are less useful.
3. **Frontmatter on every wiki page.** Required: `title`, `type`, `created`, `updated`, `tags`.
4. **Use the correct page type.** Match content to the five defined types.
5. **Discuss before filing.** Present takeaways to the user before creating pages during ingest.
6. **Cascade updates.** When ingesting, review and update all related existing pages.
7. **Categorize the index.** Group entries by topic domain, not flat chronological.
8. **Heading-level log entries.** Use `## [YYYY-MM-DD] type | description` format.
9. **Maintain the working set.** Keep `context.md` current with active focus areas.
10. **Skeptical memory.** Before acting on any recalled fact, verify it against the current codebase or source. Memory is a hint, not truth.
11. **Atomic pages.** One concept per wiki page. If a page covers multiple topics, split it.
12. **Maintain backlinks.** Every wiki page has a `## Backlinks` section at the bottom listing pages that link to it, with context. Update backlinks on the target page whenever you create or update a wikilink.

lastPrompt: Can we review the Obsidian compatible frontmatter to make sure its correct, and we can load a wiki into Obsidian and work properly

content.text:
I rendered live samples of the three frontmatter formats Cairn produces (wiki page, session manifest, session summary) and checked them against Obsidian's frontmatter rules.

## Verdict

**Wiki pages: fully Obsidian-compatible.** Drop `~/cairn` into Obsidian and they will render correctly with typed Properties, working tags, working backlinks, and working wikilinks.

**Session manifests: load fine but won't render in the Properties UI** because the YAML contains nested objects. Not broken — just sparse.

**Session summaries: fine.**

## What works

| Field | Used in | Obsidian rendering |
|---|---|---|
| `title`, `type`, `source` | wiki | Text property |
| `tags: [architecture/layering, go/echo]` | wiki | List, nested via `/` ✅ |
| `aliases: [...]` | wiki | List ✅ — note alias-search works |
| `created: 2026-04-26` / `updated:` | wiki | Date type (date picker) ✅ |
| `source: "[[raw/foo.md]]"` | wiki | Quoted wikilink — Obsidian indexes it for backlinks ✅ |
| Body wikilinks `[[Page]]`, `[[Page#Heading]]`, `![[Page]]`, `> [!info]` callouts | wiki/log | All standard Obsidian syntax ✅ |
| `.cairn/`, `sessions/.trash/` | hidden dirs | Hidden from Obsidian explorer ✅ |
| `checkVaultState` detects pre-existing `.obsidian/` | init | Refuses to clobber an Obsidian vault ✅ |

## Issues worth fixing

1. **`timestamp` and `generated_at` use `…Z` suffix** (`2026-04-26T22:57:31Z`). Obsidian's datetime type expects `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS` with no `Z`. With the `Z`, Obsidian falls back to plain text in Properties. Cosmetic, but easy: emit `.toISOString().slice(0, 19)` for these fields, or just accept text rendering.

2. **Session manifest has nested objects in frontmatter** (`excerpt: { head, tail }`, `files_changed: [{ path, action }]`). Obsidian Properties UI shows "1 unsupported property" and hides them — flat fields (session_id, branch, etc.) still render. The file still parses; it just can't be edited in the Properties pane. If you want manifests to be readable in Obsidian, move `excerpt` and `files_changed` into the body as fenced YAML/markdown.

3. **Manifests have an empty body.** They appear as blank pages with only Properties. Adding a one-line body ("Session manifest — see properties.") would make them less jarring in the editor.

4. **`index.md`, `log.md`, `context.md`, `CAIRN.md` have no frontmatter.** Optional polish: add `tags: [cairn/index]` etc. so they're filterable from Obsidian's tag pane.

## Things that are fine, not breaking

- `type: concept | entity | …` — Obsidian has no Select type, but renders as Text with autocomplete from sibling pages. Works.
- Empty arrays in manifests (`tags: []`, `decisions: []`) — render as empty list inputs, harmless.
- Filenames use `-` instead of `:` — cross-platform safe.
- `raw/` is visible in Obsidian's explorer, which is correct (sources are linked from summaries).

Want me to patch the `Z`-suffix datetimes and add a minimal body to manifests so they render cleanly in Obsidian?

# Activities vs Events (GameRight)

Core architectural principle inside [[Dionysus (GameRight)]]: strict separation between **template** (Activity) and **instance** (Event).

## The split

- **Game** — a tool stored in [[Atlas (GameRight)]] (e.g., Counter-Strike 2)
- **Activity** — a specific method of play: rules, objectives, configuration (e.g., "CS2 5v5 Bomb Defusal Tournament Format")
- **Event** — one live occurrence of an Activity: dates, participants, results (e.g., "Ohio CS2 Spring Cup — 2026-05-15")

# Lint the Cairn Vault

Invoke the `cairn` skill and follow its Lint workflow. Report findings only —
all fixes are opt-in.

Expected output:

1. Vault health dashboard (pages, links, orphans, dead links, stale, types,
   backlinks coverage).
2. Orphan pages (no inbound links).
3. Dead links (wikilinks to non-existent pages).
4. Missing frontmatter (required: `title`, `type`, `created`, `updated`, `tags`).
5. Stale content (`updated` > 30 days).
6. Missing `type` field.
7. **Contradictions** — conflicting claims across pages. Flag both pages and
   the conflicting statements. Most dangerous vault failure mode.
8. Missing or out-of-sync `## Backlinks` sections.

Do not auto-fix without user approval.

content.text:
Found the root cause. It's a convention mismatch, not a frontmatter bug.

## What's broken

Your wiki pages have:
- Filename: `activities-vs-events-gameright.md` (kebab-case)
- Frontmatter: `title: Activities vs Events (GameRight)`
- Wikilinks in body: `[[Activities vs Events (GameRight)]]`

Obsidian resolves `[[X]]` by checking, in order:
1. A file literally named `X.md` — no match (filename is kebab-case)
2. A page whose `aliases:` contains `X` — no match (title is not in the page's own aliases)

So every title-form wikilink becomes a "click to create" link, even though the page exists. The "20 backlinks" you see are the inbound links Obsidian is correctly counting against the *phantom* page name.

I see this in your vault: some pages use the right pattern `[[actualchat-customizations|ActualChat Customizations]]` (filename + display alias) but most use `[[ActualChat Customizations]]` which doesn't resolve.

## Does cairn produce correct Obsidian links today?

**Frontmatter: yes.** **Link convention: no.** The current `CAIRN.md` template (lines 199-209) tells the agent to write `[[Page Name]]` style wikilinks, but `init` doesn't dictate filename style and the agent has been creating kebab-case filenames. There's no rule that says "title must equal filename" or "title must be in aliases", so the two drift apart.

## Is there a cairn CLI command to fix this?

No. The `lint` and `refine` workflows are agent instructions in `CAIRN.md` — they get executed by Claude, not by a `cairn` subcommand. There's no `cairn lint` or `cairn fix-links` binary today.

## Two fixes, both worth doing

**Fix 1 — one-time backfill of existing vault.** Add the `title` to each page's `aliases:` array (idempotent — skip if already present). After this, Obsidian resolves every existing `[[Title-form]]` wikilink correctly without rewriting any prose. ~172 files, maybe 5 seconds. Reversible.

**Fix 2 — update the template so cairn produces resolvable links going forward.** Two options:
- **A.** Change `CAIRN.md` to require: when creating a page, include the page's `title` in `aliases:` (so kebab-case filenames stay, and title-form wikilinks always resolve).
- **B.** Change the link convention to always use `[[kebab-filename|Display Title]]` (the form some of your pages already use). More verbose; works without aliases.

I'd recommend **A** — minimal change to the template, matches the existing pattern most of your pages have already converged on, and keeps prose readable.

Want me to:
1. Write a small one-shot script to do Fix 1 across `~/cairn/wiki/` (preview first, apply on confirm), and
2. Patch `CAIRN.md` for Fix 1A?

content.text:
Good call. Let me lay out the combined plan, then confirm before touching anything.

## Plan

**1. New CLI command: `cairn migrate-links`** (mirrors `migrate-sessions` pattern — preview by default, `--apply --yes` to commit)

What it does, for every wikilink in `wiki/*.md`, `index.md`, `log.md`, `context.md`:

| Input | Output |
|---|---|
| `[[Activities vs Events (GameRight)]]` | `[[activities-vs-events-gameright\|Activities vs Events (GameRight)]]` |
| `[[Activities vs Events (GameRight)#Auth Flow]]` | `[[activities-vs-events-gameright#Auth Flow\|Activities vs Events (GameRight)]]` |
| `[[Title Form\|Custom Display]]` | `[[kebab-form\|Custom Display]]` (preserve display) |
| `[[kebab-form]]` (no display) | `[[kebab-form\|Title from frontmatter]]` |
| `[[kebab-form\|Display]]` | unchanged (already correct) |
| `[[raw/notion-foo.md]]` | unchanged (path-style, already resolves) |
| `![[Page]]` embeds | same rules apply |

Resolution order: filename match → frontmatter `title` match → `aliases` match. If unresolved, leave alone and report as broken.

**2. Patch `templates/CAIRN.md`**

- "Markdown Conventions / Wikilinks" section: mandate `[[kebab-filename|Display Title]]` for all wiki cross-refs; `[[Title]]` form is forbidden.
- Update the page template examples to show the new form.
- Update the Ingest workflow step about linking to spell out the convention.

**3. Smaller Obsidian-compat fixes** (from earlier review):

- Strip `Z` suffix from `timestamp`/`generated_at` so Obsidian renders them as Datetime properties (no behavior change, just nicer Properties UI).
- Optional: minimal body on session manifests so they aren't blank in Obsidian. *Want this or skip?*

**4. Tests**

- Unit tests for the link rewriter (each case in the table above + ambiguity + broken targets + heading/block preservation).
- Integration test for `cairn migrate-links` preview/apply on a fixture vault.

**5. Run the migration on your `~/cairn`**

- Preview first: I'll show you the counts (rewrites, already-correct, ambiguous, unresolved) and a sample diff.
- Apply only after you confirm.
- Backups: I'll copy the affected files to `~/cairn/.cairn/migration-backups/<timestamp>/` before writing.

## Order of execution

I'd build it in this order so you can sanity-check each piece:

1. Implement rewrite logic + tests (no vault touch).
2. Wire up `cairn migrate-links` command + tests.
3. Run **preview** on `~/cairn` and show you the diff stats.
4. You approve → apply with backup.
5. Patch `CAIRN.md` template + cosmetic frontmatter fixes.
6. Verify final state.

Two questions before I start:

- **Manifest body** — add a one-line "Session manifest — see properties." body, or leave them empty? (Pure cosmetic, only affects how they look opened in Obsidian.)
- **Ambiguity policy** — if a wikilink resolves to multiple candidates (e.g., a title that matches one page's title AND another page's alias), should the migration: (a) skip and report, (b) prefer title over alias, or (c) prefer the alphabetically-first filename? I'd default to **(a) skip and report** so nothing gets silently mis-routed.

Confirm those two and I'll start with step 1.

## Markdown Conventions

### Wikilinks
Use `[[wikilinks]]` for all internal references:
- `[[Page Name]]` — link to page
- `[[Page Name|Display Text]]` — aliased link
- `[[Page Name#Heading]]` — link to heading

- Embed a page: `![[Page Name]]`
- Embed a heading: `![[Page Name#Heading]]`
- Callouts: `> [!info]`, `> [!warning]`, `> [!tip]`

## Workflows

### Ingest

When the user asks you to ingest something (file path, URL, pasted text, or conversation context):

1. Read the source from wherever it lives — a local path, URL, or inline content.
2. If the source is a file, copy it to `raw/` for provenance (preserving original filename).
3. **Present key takeaways to the user**: entities found, concepts identified, relationships discovered, and any contradictions with existing wiki content. Confirm which are worth filing before proceeding.
4. For each confirmed entity/concept, create or update a wiki page in `wiki/`.
5. **Review all existing wiki pages that relate to this source.** Update them with new cross-references, corrections, or additional context from this source. A single ingest typically touches 5-15 existing pages.
6. Link related pages with `[[wikilinks]]` — every page links to at least 2 others.
7. Update `## Backlinks` sections on all pages you linked to — add an entry with context for each new inbound link.
8. Update `context.md` if the ingested material relates to current focus areas.
9. Add entries to `index.md` under the appropriate category.
10. Append to `log.md`:

```markdown
## [YYYY-MM-DD] ingest | <source title>

## Markdown Conventions

### Wikilinks

**Canonical form: `[[kebab-filename|Display Title]]`.** The target is always
the filename (without `.md`); the display is the human-readable title from
that page's frontmatter. Obsidian resolves wikilinks by filename match — using
the title in the target slot creates phantom pages even when the file exists.

| Form | Use |
|------|-----|
| `[[kebab-filename\|Display Title]]` | Standard link |
| `[[kebab-filename#Heading\|Display Title]]` | Link to a heading |
| `[[kebab-filename#^block-id\|Display Title]]` | Link to a block |
| `[[raw/filename.md]]` | Path-style link to a `raw/` source (no display) |

Display text is required on every wikilink to a wiki page. Omitting the
display text shows the raw filename in the rendered page, which reads poorly.

### Embeds and Callouts
- Embed a page: `![[kebab-filename]]`
- Embed a heading: `![[kebab-filename#Heading]]`
- Callouts: `> [!info]`, `> [!warning]`, `> [!tip]`

### Filenames

Wiki pages use lowercase kebab-case filenames: letters, digits, and hyphens.
No spaces, no parentheses, no slashes. The `title` field in frontmatter
carries the human-readable name; the filename stays stable and link-safe.

   ```

   ```
2. **Orphan pages**: wiki pages with no inbound links from other wiki pages or index.md.
3. **Dead links**: `[[wikilinks]]` pointing to non-existent pages.
4. **Missing frontmatter**: wiki pages without required YAML frontmatter fields (`title`, `type`, `created`, `updated`, `tags`).
5. **Stale content**: pages with `updated` date older than 30 days.
6. **Missing types**: wiki pages without a valid `type` field.

## Index Format

`index.md` groups pages by topic category, newest first within each category:

```markdown
# Vault Index

## Architecture
- [[Layered Architecture]] — Handler → Service → Repo/Gateway boundaries
- [[Dependency Injection]] — Constructor-based DI pattern for Go services

## Tools
- [[React 19]] — New features: use hook, actions, compiler
- [[Stripe]] — Payment gateway integration patterns
```

Categories emerge organically during ingest. Create new categories as needed. Each entry: `- [[Page Name]] — one-line description` (~150 chars max).

## Log Format

`log.md` uses heading-level entries for Obsidian folding and CLI parsing:

```markdown
## [YYYY-MM-DD] type | description

Details of what was created/updated.
```

Types: `ingest`, `query`, `lint`, `refine`, `session` (lowercase).

## Working Set

`context.md` tracks current focus areas. The inject hook reads this first — it gets highest priority in the context budget.

```markdown
# Working Set

## Active
- [[Page Name]] — why it matters right now

## Background
- [[Page Name]] — reference material for active work
```

Update `context.md` when:
- The user starts or finishes a major work area
- Ingested material relates to current focus
- The user explicitly asks to shift focus

## Search

When [qmd](https://github.com/qntx-labs/qmd) is available (via MCP tools `qmd_search`, `qmd_deep_search`, `qmd_get`), use it as the primary search mechanism for Query and Refine workflows.

### Setup (user responsibility)

1. Install qmd: `npm install -g @tobilu/qmd`
2. Register the vault: `qmd collection add ~/cairn --name cairn --mask "**/*.md"`
3. Generate embeddings: `qmd embed`
4. Add MCP server to Claude Code config:
   ```json
   {
     "mcpServers": {
       "qmd": { "command": "qmd", "args": ["mcp"] }
     }
   }
   ```

### Usage in workflows

Before each workflow, check your actual tool list for `mcp__qmd__qmd_search`,
`mcp__qmd__qmd_deep_search`, and `mcp__qmd__qmd_get` (or the non-prefixed
`qmd_*` form). Decide from presence, not from memory.

When the tools are present:
- **Query**: Use `qmd_deep_search` first, then `qmd_get` for top results, then follow wikilinks.
- **Refine**: Use `qmd_search` to find pages with overlapping content (merge candidates).
- **Ingest cascade**: Use `qmd_search` to find related pages that need updating.

When the tools are absent, fall back to reading `index.md` and following
wikilinks manually. Announce the fallback once per session. qmd is optional —
the vault works without it.

## Rules

1. **Never modify files in `raw/`.** They are archived originals preserved for provenance.
2. **Every wiki page links to 2+ related pages.** Isolated pages are less useful.
3. **Frontmatter on every wiki page.** Required: `title`, `type`, `created`, `updated`, `tags`.
4. **Use the correct page type.** Match content to the five defined types.
5. **Discuss before filing.** Present takeaways to the user before creating pages during ingest.
6. **Cascade updates.** When ingesting, review and update all related existing pages.
7. **Categorize the index.** Group entries by topic domain, not flat chronological.
8. **Heading-level log entries.** Use `## [YYYY-MM-DD] type | description` format.
9. **Maintain the working set.** Keep `context.md` current with active focus areas.
10. **Skeptical memory.** Before acting on any recalled fact, verify it against the current codebase or source. Memory is a hint, not truth.
11. **Atomic pages.** One concept per wiki page. If a page covers multiple topics, split it.
12. **Maintain backlinks.** Every wiki page has a `## Backlinks` section at the bottom listing pages that link to it, with context. Update backlinks on the target page whenever you create or update a wikilink.

# Cairn

A persistent memory plugin for Claude Code. Markdown vault maintained by
your agent across sessions. Based on [Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern](https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f).

## What it does

- **SessionStart hook** injects working set, index, and recent session summaries or manifests (priority order)
- **Stop hook** writes a small session manifest when you finish working
- **PostCompact hook** re-injects vault context after compaction (solves compaction amnesia)
- **CAIRN.md template** teaches Claude to ingest sources, answer queries, and lint your vault

## Install

```bash
bunx cairn init
```

Scaffolds `~/cairn` with vault structure and registers hooks in Claude Code.
Safe to run again — idempotent.

### Custom vault path

```bash
bunx cairn init --vault-path /path/to/vault
```

### Per-project vault

Create a `.cairn` file in your project root containing the vault path:

```
/path/to/project/vault
```

## Usage

### Ingest
Give Claude any source — file path, URL, pasted text, or conversation context:

> "Ingest this article on dependency injection"

Claude reads source, copies files to `raw/` for provenance, presents
takeaways for confirmation, writes wiki pages, cascades updates to related
pages, updates index and log. Single ingest typically touches 5-15 pages.

### Query
Ask Claude anything your vault might know:

> "What decisions did we make about the auth system?"

Claude checks index, follows wikilinks, answers with citations. Novel
insights get filed as new wiki pages automatically.

### Lint
Ask Claude to check vault health:

> "Lint the vault"

Reports orphan pages, dead links, missing frontmatter, stale content,
missing types, and contradictions.

### Refine
Ask Claude to improve vault structure:

> "Refine the vault"

Finds stale pages, under-connected pages, merge/split candidates, and
backlink gaps. Presents findings for approval, then applies changes.
Shows vault health before and after.

### Extract
Extract knowledge from session manifests into wiki pages:

> "Extract from sessions"

Sessions are sources. Claude asks `cairn summarize` to lazily create cached
summaries for unprocessed manifests, presents extraction candidates (entities,
concepts, decisions, patterns), and runs the standard ingest workflow on
confirmed items.

Toggle session-start nudge: `/cairn:extract on` or `/cairn:extract off`.

## Page types

Every wiki page has a `type` in frontmatter:

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `concept` | Ideas, techniques, patterns |
| `entity` | People, orgs, tools, projects |
| `source-summary` | Digest of a `raw/` document |
| `comparison` | X vs Y trade-off analysis |
| `overview` | Guided index of a topic area |

Each type has a structural template defined in CAIRN.md with recommended sections.

## Vault structure

```
~/cairn/
  CAIRN.md        # Schema — conventions, workflows, rules
  context.md      # Working set — current focus areas (injected first)
  index.md        # Categorized pointer index, grouped by topic
  log.md          # Chronological operation log (heading-level entries)
  wiki/           # Knowledge pages (agent-maintained, typed)
  raw/            # Source documents (immutable, provenance)
  sessions/       # Session manifests (auto-generated)
    summaries/    # Cached summaries from manifests
    .trash/       # Migration quarantine and non-destructive replacements
```

## Context injection

Inject hook reads vault in priority order under a 32KB budget (configurable
via `CAIRN_BUDGET` env var; `cairn doctor` warns when the budget is under
pressure):

1. **`context.md`** — working set, always first
2. **`index.md`** — categorized page index
3. **Recent sessions** — cached summaries first, then manifests as fallback

## Session migration

Cairn v0.6.0 stores session manifests separately from cached summaries. Existing
vaults can preview and apply the one-time migration:

```bash
bunx cairn migrate-sessions
bunx cairn migrate-sessions --apply --yes
```

`cairn doctor` warns when legacy session files remain and reports manifest,
summary, and trash counts.

## Search (optional)

For larger vaults, [qmd](https://github.com/qntx-labs/qmd) provides
BM25 + vector hybrid search over markdown files.

```bash
# Install
npm install -g @tobilu/qmd

# Register vault
qmd collection add ~/cairn --name cairn --mask "**/*.md"
qmd embed

# Add MCP server to Claude Code
# In your Claude Code MCP config:
# { "mcpServers": { "qmd": { "command": "qmd", "args": ["mcp"] } } }
```

When qmd MCP tools are available, Query and Refine workflows use
`qmd_deep_search` before falling back to manual index reading.

## Model choice

Stop hooks do not call an LLM. Summaries are generated on demand by
`cairn summarize`, which calls `claude -p --model haiku` by default. For tests
or custom integrations, set `CAIRN_SUMMARIZE_COMMAND` to a compatible command.

## Uninstall

```bash
bunx cairn uninstall
```

Removes hooks from Claude Code. Your vault is preserved.

## License

MIT
